title: Portfolio Website
description: Modern portfolio built with React and Firebase

architectureCaseStudy.diagramBasic:
flowchart LR
  subgraph Client[Browser]
    SPA[Vite + React SPA]
  end
  subgraph Data
    FB[(Firestore catalog)]
    JSON[Static JSON fallback]
  end
  SPA --> FB
  SPA --> JSON
architectureCaseStudy.diagramAdvanced:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  actor Visitor
  participant CDN as Static CDN
  participant SPA as Vite React SPA
  participant SDK as Firestore client
  participant Rules as Security rules
  participant DB as Firestore DB
  Visitor->>CDN: Request app shell
  CDN-->>Visitor: Cached static bundles
  Visitor->>SPA: Use portfolio UI
  alt Firebase configured
    SPA->>SDK: Query subscribe projects
    SDK->>Rules: Authorize read write
    Rules->>DB: Allowed operations only
    DB-->>SDK: Project documents
    SDK-->>SPA: Catalog data
  else No Firebase dev fallback
    SPA->>SPA: Import static JSON catalog
  end
  SPA-->>Visitor: Render grid and filters
architectureCaseStudy.stackRationaleBasic: Firestore fits a read-mostly project catalog with optional live updates and no dedicated backend VM to operate. A checked-in JSON fallback keeps the site usable when Firebase env vars are missing—important for contributors and CI.
architectureCaseStudy.stackRationaleAdvanced: A relational DB would add connection pooling, migrations, and an API layer for the same read patterns. Here, document reads map naturally to project rows, rules enforce auth on writes, and the SPA stays serverless. SQL would shine for complex reporting or cross-table joins we do not need yet.
architectureCaseStudy.scalabilityBasic: Traffic scales with static assets on the CDN; Firestore handles concurrent readers on the catalog collection. Writes are rare (portfolio updates), so hot paths stay read-optimized.
architectureCaseStudy.scalabilityAdvanced: If catalog growth or traffic spikes: add composite indexes for filtered queries, paginate list endpoints client-side, and consider a tiny Cloud Function only if we need server-side aggregation. For global latency, rules + regional Firestore and immutable hashed asset URLs keep TTFB predictable.
performanceDecisions: Code-split Mermaid and heavy routes so the initial bundle stays lean for LCP.
Prefer transform/opacity animations over layout thrash; honor prefers-reduced-motion.
Firestore reads batched where possible; static JSON path avoids network entirely in dev.
Security rules as the authoritative gate instead of duplicating checks only in UI.
architectureCaseStudy.cicdBasic: Push triggers GitHub Actions to lint and build; static files deploy to your host (Pages, Vercel, or S3+CDN). No long-running server in the pipeline.
architectureCaseStudy.cicdAdvanced: Branch protection + required checks before merge; preview deployments per PR when the host supports it. Secrets for Firebase live in CI only for production deploys; local dev can skip Firebase via the JSON catalog.

challenges: Keeping Firebase optional so local dev works without .env
Filtering and sorting a mixed Firestore + static catalog
